blockingIssues.description: apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/git.rs:1272 has a simple-push test that asserts `result.commits_pushed == 0`, which contradicts the requirement that successful pushes report an accurate non-zero pushed count for that scenario.

apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/git.rs:1271 still encodes incorrect simple-push expectations by asserting both `result.commits_pushed == 0` and `!status.has_remote` after a successful initial push, so the integration suite can pass while preserving broken post-push semantics.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the tauri-fullstack skill to document the temporary local repo + bare remote fixture pattern for validating git CLI workflows like smart push, and explicitly call out key postconditions such as `commits_pushed` and `has_remote` that those tests should assert.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both failing reviews on smart push identified incorrect or missing ship() workflow assertions, and one reviewer noted the skill asks for ship() tests without specifying the fixture pattern or critical postconditions.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify in tauri-fullstack that verification-only reruns or non-visual config migrations may skip browser/manual UI validation when the change does not affect a browser-testable surface.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Pass reviews for ai-settings-simplify and fix-gemini-config-migration both noted the skill's default browser-verification procedure did not fit already-landed or non-visual config-only work, causing explicit deviations.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add a library note right now for bare-remote git fixture patterns.
rejectedObservations.reason: Rejected because the current round surfaced incorrect assertions in the first implementation of those tests; guidance should not be promoted to shared reference material until a correct, validated pattern exists in the codebase.

rejectedObservations.observation: Document the stale `gpt-5.3-codex-spark` mock value in shared library docs.
rejectedObservations.reason: Rejected because this is isolated mock-data drift rather than durable architectural knowledge, and documenting it would not improve future worker execution.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add ConfigContext AI normalization guidance to the library immediately.
rejectedObservations.reason: Rejected because the migration behavior is feature-specific and already reflected in code; no repeated cross-feature confusion was shown in this scrutiny round.
